title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
The Web Storage API provides localStorage and sessionStorage for storing key-value pairs in the browser.

localStorage:
• Persists indefinitely (until manually cleared or user clears browser data).
• Shared across all tabs of the same origin.
• Storage limit: ~5–10 MB.

sessionStorage:
• Cleared when the tab/window is closed.
• Not shared between tabs.

Both only store strings — use JSON.stringify/parse for objects.

Methods: .setItem(key, val), .getItem(key), .removeItem(key), .clear(), .key(i), .length.
